name: My respiratory symptoms started months after work exposure, is it too late?

mainEntity.name: My respiratory symptoms started months after work exposure, is it too late?

acceptedAnswer.text: File within 1-3 years of diagnosis; prove medical link despite delay.
acceptedAnswer.description:
**No, but file within your state's statute for occupational diseases, often 1-3 years from diagnosis.**

Workers' comp accepts delayed-onset illnesses like lung damage if medically tied to exposure[2][3]. Utah allows claims up to 3 years post-diagnosis for occupational illness[1].

- Document work history, chemicals, timelines.
- Doctor opinion on causation critical.
- No strict 'discovery' rule everywhere.

See a doctor ASAP for records; attorney ensures timely filing. Delays weaken cases[1][8].

description: File within 1-3 years of diagnosis; prove medical link despite delay.
